perturbation.original_text: Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refer s to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient with clear label or designation of "Confidential information" (here inafter referred to as "Confidential information"), excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be make public available, except those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Re cipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization.
perturbation.changed_text: Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refers to data and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient (hereinafter referred to as "Confidential information"), excluding the following: Technical information or design concepts that are already publicly available.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for a 'clear label or designation of "Confidential information"' to qualify as confidential information, the text contradicts later sections where the process of identifying information as confidential is assumed. The change creates ambiguity about what information is actually protected under the agreement. For example, the sentence 'Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information...' is now uncertain because it's unclear what constitutes 'confidential information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'clear label or designation' for information to be considered confidential. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text states that 'Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information'. Since the changed text no longer requires a label, it is now unclear what information is protected, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text which assumes that confidential information is clearly defined and identifiable. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose confidential information to a third party without the written approval or authorization of the other party. Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose confidential information to a third party. Unless the disclosure of confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the cooperative programs under normal circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information', we are omitting a restriction on how the confidential information can be used. This creates a contradiction with the implicit expectation that the information will not only be kept secret but also not used for purposes other than the cooperative programs. This contradicts Article 2.1 that states 'Both Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient(Party B) have the right to preserve necessary confidential information, so as to make use of which in implementing bindi ng laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient(Party B) have the right to preserve necessary confidential information, so as to make use of which in implementing bindi ng laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the use of confidential information, preventing its disclosure, publication, or any other use without approval. The changed text removes the specific restriction against 'otherwise make use of the confidential information'. Article 2.1 states that Entrusting party or Recipient party have the right to preserve necessary confidential information, so as to make use of which in implementing binding laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs. The omission contradicts Article 2.1 by removing a restriction on the use of confidential information, creating a contradiction with the implicit expectation that the information will not only be kept secret but also not used for purposes other than the cooperative programs. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The following items are not covered by the concept of this Confidential Information: (i) Information in the public domain at the effective date of the Confidentiality Agreement or information which becomes part of the public domain other than through non- compliance with the Confidentiality Agreement. (ii) Informatio n that was already in the Recipient's possession before the Recipient received it from Entrusting party. (iii) Information that the Recipient has obtained from a third party without any obligation to keep the information secret. (iv) Information that the Recipient ha s obtained by unlawful means from and out of the domain of either parties beyond despite the due care and diligence exercised by either parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The following items are not covered by this Confidential Information: (i) Information in the public domain. (ii) Informatio n that was already in the Recipient's possession before the Recipient received it from Entrusting party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting exclusions (iii) and (iv), the agreement now treats information obtained from a third party without confidentiality obligations, or information obtained unlawfully, as confidential. This is an in-text contradiction because it conflicts with standard confidentiality practices and the implied understanding that only genuinely sensitive information should be protected. This contradicts with article 3.1 'Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient (Party B )to the other party or its representatives sha ll not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives of the rights and interests in relation to its trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how or any other intellectual property'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient (Party B )to the other party or its representatives sha ll not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives of the rights and interests in relation to its trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how or any other intellectual property , nor shall it constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives the rights and interests in relation to the trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how, or any other intellectual property authorized by a third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits exclusions regarding confidential information, specifically (iii) Information that the Recipient has obtained from a third party without any obligation to keep the information secret and (iv) Information that the Recipient has obtained by unlawful means. This means that under the changed text, information obtained from a third party without confidentiality obligations, or information obtained unlawfully, could now be considered confidential. The contradicted text states that 'Disclosure of the confidential information...authorized by a third party' does not grant rights or interests in relation to trade secrets. However, if information obtained unlawfully from a third party is now considered confidential under the changed text, it creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text. The original text prevented this conflict. This falls under Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days or upon the request of the other party at any time in writing duly addressed to the party opting out of this agreement. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program, a party shall destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for record purposes, without breaching other provisions of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'with reasons' now means that even if a party quits for a breach of contract by the other party, they still have to return the confidential information, this contradicts the principles of justice and fairness. Also, changing 'for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder' to 'for record purposes' broadens the allowable use of the retained information, potentially allowing actions that would breach confidentiality. This contradicts with article 4.1 that says: 'This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.justification: The change removes 'with reasons', obligating a party to return confidential information even if the other party breached the agreement. This conflicts with the legal principles implied in the contradicted text: 'This agreement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordance with the laws of the People's Republic of China'. Enforcing the return of confidential information after a breach could be seen as unfair under the law. Also, changing 'for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder' to 'for record purposes' broadens the allowable use of the retained information, potentially allowing actions that would breach confidentiality, thus contradicting article 4.1.

perturbation.original_text: Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient (Party B )to the other party or its representatives sha ll not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives of the rights and interests in relation to its trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how or any other intellectual property , nor shall it constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives the rights and interests in relation to the trade secrets , trademarks , patents , know -how, or any other intellectual property authorized by a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Disclosure of the confidential information by either Entrusting party or Recipient to the other party or its representatives shall not be construed to constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives of the rights and interests in relation to its trade secrets, trademarks, patents, know-how or any other intellectual property.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'nor shall it constitute an assignment or grant to the other party or its representatives the rights and interests in relation to the trade secrets, trademarks, patents, know-how, or any other intellectual property authorized by a third party', the modified text introduces uncertainty regarding the ownership of intellectual property. The text now contradicts implied expectation that the recipient of the confidential information will not gain any rights or interests to the entrusting party's third-party licensed IP. Also, now this contradicts with Article 2.1 where it says 'Both Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient(Party B) have the right to preserve necessary confidential information, so as to make use of which in implementing bindi ng laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient(Party B) have the right to preserve necessary confidential information, so as to make use of which in implementing bindi ng laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that disclosure of confidential information does not grant rights to the other party regarding intellectual property, including that authorized by a third party. The changed text removes this explicit exclusion of third-party authorized IP. This omission creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, 'Both Entrusting party(Party A) or Recipient(Party B) have the right to preserve necessary confidential information, so as to make use of which in implementing bindi ng laws, regulations, and obligations under their cooperative programs,' because it introduces uncertainty about whether the recipient now gains rights or interests regarding the entrusting party's third-party licensed IP, impacting their ability to use the confidential information under cooperative programs. This directly contradicts the original clear statement preventing such a transfer of rights.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be terminated for the following reasons; i) by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement; ii) by mutual consent wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party; iii) breach of the covenants by either parties iv) outbreak of war, violence, or emergency between the two countries, under the circumstance, both the parties are at liberty to either suspend the agreement temporarily and revive the same on restoration of peace between the two countries or permanently terminate the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be terminated for the following reasons; i) by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement; ii) by mutual consent; iii) breach of the covenants by either parties iv) outbreak of war, violence, or emergency between the two countries.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party' and 'under the circumstance, both the parties are at liberty to either suspend the agreement temporarily and revive the same on restoration of peace between the two countries or permanently terminate the agreement', the ability for parties to suspend or delay the agreement due to circumstances is lost. It is no longer in compliance with Chinese Contract Law that allows parties to suspend contractual obligations during force majeure events. This contradicts article 4.1 that says 'This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China.'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for suspension or temporary termination of the agreement due to war, violence, or emergency. The changed text removes this option, creating a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states that the agreement is governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China. According to the explanation, Chinese Contract Law allows for suspension of contractual obligations during force majeure events, which is now impossible under the changed text. This creates a strong contradiction because the changed text removes a right afforded under the governing law as stated in the contradicted text.
